insights.text: becoming comfortable in the unknown present moment allows for true creativity, moving beyond programmed survival instincts.
insights.quote: Being in the present moment, truly in the present moment, is being comfortable in the unknown...The present moment is the unknown because there is the familiar past that we feel emotionally and we have the predictable future, which are both the knowns.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Dr. Joe Dispenza: Secret To Living Without Fear & Anxiety Forever! Your Mind Can Heal Itself! Stress Is The Root Of 90% Of All Diseases!

insights.text: to change, one must be greater than their body, environment, and perception of time.
insights.quote: To change is to be greater than the conditions in your environment, to be able to think, act and feel differently in your same environment.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Dr. Joe Dispenza: Secret To Living Without Fear & Anxiety Forever! Your Mind Can Heal Itself! Stress Is The Root Of 90% Of All Diseases!

insights.text: creatine is a valuable supplement, not just for muscle growth, but also for brain health.
insights.quote: It's been shown to actually improve brain health and performance in sleep deprived and in high stress states.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Physiotherapist: You Need To Know This About Creatine! Melt Belly Fat With One Change! The Hidden Cost Of Getting Shredded! - Jeff Cavaliere

insights.text: creating 'emotional buttons' helps reconnect with positive emotions and manage anxiety by identifying triggers that lead to feelings of peace and happiness.
insights.quote: I need a place where I canget back to that. And I need that formula. So I call these emotional buttons.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Matthew Hussey: The Secret To Building A Perfect Relationship

insights.text: confronting regrets involves three steps: treating oneself with kindness, expressing outward through disclosure, and extracting a lesson by getting some remove.
insights.quote: Think of it as inward outward forward... Treat yourself with kindness rather than contempt... Express outward, make sense of it through disclosure and language, and extract a lesson from it by getting some remove.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Real Trick To Long Term Motivation: Daniel Pink

insights.text: remembering small details about people and bringing them up later creates a strong positive impression.
insights.quote: Do you know how great that feeling is to somebody when you remember things they told you? It's like winning the lottery.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Oz Pearlman (Mentalist): This Small Mistake Makes People Dislike You! They Do This, They’re Lying!

insights.text: focusing your attention determines what others will remember; guide their focus to create the desired narrative.
insights.quote: It doesn't matter what I do. It matters what people remember. and what's the story they tell others.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Oz Pearlman (Mentalist): This Small Mistake Makes People Dislike You! They Do This, They’re Lying!

insights.text: in long-term relationships, spontaneous desire fades, requiring active creation through engaging sexual experiences.
insights.quote: When you get into a long-term relationship, desire doesn't tap you on the shoulder anymore. You have to create it.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 128: How To Fix Your Sexless Relationship: Tracey Cox

insights.text: micro-steps are a powerful tool for behavior change, creating a sense of success and avoiding the shame associated with abandoned resolutions.
insights.quote: The alternative is what we're doing, which is micro steps, too small to fail.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Arianna Huffington: I Was Lying In A Little Pool Of Blood! The Huffington Post, A $1 Billion 'Flop'!

insights.text: hiring the wrong person can ruin a great culture. if you have any inkling that something is wrong with a hire, listen to your intuition.
insights.quote: It's so much more important not to hire the wrong person than to miss someone.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Arianna Huffington: I Was Lying In A Little Pool Of Blood! The Huffington Post, A $1 Billion 'Flop'!

insights.text: people often relive miserable lives they never had, using their inaccurate past as an excuse to avoid change.
insights.quote: 50% of the story we tell in our past isn't even the truth. That means that people are reliving a miserable life they never even had just to excuse themselves from changing.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Dr. Joe Dispenza: Secret To Living Without Fear & Anxiety Forever! Your Mind Can Heal Itself! Stress Is The Root Of 90% Of All Diseases!

insights.text: young people should prioritize finding a career with a high employment rate where they can be in the top 10% rather than chasing passion in a field with low economic prospects.
insights.quote: Once you find something you're really good at, that you could be in the top 1% at... that has a 90 plus percent employment rate... you're going to make really good money.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Investing Expert: We’re Raising The Most Unhappy Generation In History! The Unseen Link Between Marriage & Wealth! Hard Work Doesn't Build Wealth! - Scott Galloway

insights.text: for young people, geographic flexibility provides a competitive advantage.
insights.quote: You have geographic flexibility. Lean into your advantages, your flexibility, your ability to recover, your ability to workshop.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Investing Expert: We’re Raising The Most Unhappy Generation In History! The Unseen Link Between Marriage & Wealth! Hard Work Doesn't Build Wealth! - Scott Galloway

insights.text: successful people work hard and smart; hard work alone is not sufficient, but it is a prerequisite for success.
insights.quote: You have to work hard, but you also have to work smart...but I've never met anyone who was genuinely successful that wasn't hardworking.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 44 - Ben Francis: Knowing This Will Accelerate Your Business

insights.text: someone becomes 'the one' not by inherent destiny but by the quality of the relationship built together through effort.
insights.quote: Someone becomes the one by what we build with them. Any commitment long-term requires true effort.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Matthew Hussey: The Secret To Building A Perfect Relationship

insights.text: disconnection from results occurs when driven solely by ego, leading to a feeling of 'never enough' and ultimately, emptiness.
insights.quote: There is the danger of just constant comparison, nothing ever being enough. And ultimately, anytime you do get something you think might be enough, you feel completely and utterly disconnected from the result after the initial high of it,which is no different from a drug fix.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Matthew Hussey: The Secret To Building A Perfect Relationship

insights.text: vulnerability, especially for men, can be perceived as weakness due to societal expectations, but it's crucial for building deeper connections and attracting genuine relationships.
insights.quote: And finally,thank you for teaching me how to lovein a way that I was too scared to before.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Matthew Hussey: The Secret To Building A Perfect Relationship

insights.text: knowing what someone is thinking provides a tactical advantage in negotiations, relationships, and career advancement.
insights.quote: If you can know what somebody's thinking...when you ask your boss for a raise or when you ask a girl or a guy out on a date. You knowing that is a huge tactical advantage.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Oz Pearlman (Mentalist): This Small Mistake Makes People Dislike You! They Do This, They’re Lying!

insights.text: the most interesting person is the most interested person in the room; ask questions that make people think, not autopilot.
insights.quote: The most interesting person in a room tends to be the most interested person in the room...Challenge yourself to be the outlier. And think of a question you can ask someone...that throws them out of autopilot.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Oz Pearlman (Mentalist): This Small Mistake Makes People Dislike You! They Do This, They’re Lying!

insights.text: remembering small details about people and bringing them up later creates a strong positive impression.
insights.quote: Do you know how great that feeling is to somebody when you remember things they told you? It's like winning the lottery.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Oz Pearlman (Mentalist): This Small Mistake Makes People Dislike You! They Do This, They’re Lying!

insights.text: instead of mourning what you don't have, focus on mastering what you do have by striving to become the best chef with the ingredients you have been given.
insights.quote: Don't aspire to have the best ingredients. Aspire to be the best chef.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Matthew Hussey: The Secret To Building A Perfect Relationship

insights.text: there is a difference between 'settling for' something and 'settling on' something; settling 'on' something comes from a place of empowerment and focusing energy into making that decision an extraordinary one.
insights.quote: Settling on says,I'm going to put my focus and my energy on something. And it's going to be extraordinary because I'm going to make it extraordinary.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Matthew Hussey: The Secret To Building A Perfect Relationship

insights.text: exercise has immediate and long-term benefits for brain function, including mood, focus, and memory.
insights.quote: Even just one workout can immediately improve your mood and focus for the next two hours, and repeated exercise changes the brain's physiology for the better.
insights.episode_title: _summary

insights.text: different types of exercise can target different brain functions; cardio improves mood and focus, while strength training enhances long-term memory.
insights.quote: Cardio is fantastic for improving mood and your ability to focus, while incorporating some strength training can be particularly beneficial for long-term memory formation.
insights.episode_title: _summary

insights.text: mindfulness practices, such as meditation, can complement exercise by further enhancing focus and reducing stress.
insights.quote: Combining physical exercise with mindfulness techniques amplifies the benefits for both cognitive function and emotional well-being.
insights.episode_title: _summary

insights.text: the number one secret to success is making it about them, not you; focus on what the other person is missing and tailor your approach accordingly.
insights.quote: Number one rule...It's not about you. It's always about them. That's been the number one secret to my success.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Oz Pearlman (Mentalist): This Small Mistake Makes People Dislike You! They Do This, They’re Lying!

insights.text: overcoming the fear of rejection is a superpower that enables you to pursue your goals with singular focus.
insights.quote: If you can get over that, it is a superpower in life...Because I stopped thinking about it not working.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Oz Pearlman (Mentalist): This Small Mistake Makes People Dislike You! They Do This, They’re Lying!

insights.quote: It doesn't matter what I do. It matters what people remember. and what's the story they tell others.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Oz Pearlman (Mentalist): This Small Mistake Makes People Dislike You! They Do This, They’re Lying!

insights.text: when building a business, it is important to build in time for reflection and joy.
insights.quote: It isn't just about achieving. It's about joy while you're achieving.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Arianna Huffington: I Was Lying In A Little Pool Of Blood! The Huffington Post, A $1 Billion 'Flop'!

insights.text: a critical but often overlooked skill is being able to effectively communicate with others
insights.quote: The greatest skill you can develop or that you would want your kids to have that will stand the test of time is storytelling.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Investing Expert: We’re Raising The Most Unhappy Generation In History! The Unseen Link Between Marriage & Wealth! Hard Work Doesn't Build Wealth! - Scott Galloway

insights.text: smart work, such as leveraging opportunities for increased revenue, is essential for rapid growth.
insights.quote: There were periods where we were finding out ways to manipulate Google in a way that got Gymshark to the top,which gave us huge revenue for next to no cost, right?
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 44 - Ben Francis: Knowing This Will Accelerate Your Business

insights.text: regret clarifies values and instructs future actions; ignoring it hinders personal growth.
insights.quote: What I want to do is actually listen to those regrets because regret does two things for us. It clarifies and it instructs...It's telling me what I value... And it's also instructing me what to do next.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Real Trick To Long Term Motivation: Daniel Pink

insights.text: failure, when viewed as a learning experience and confronted deliberately, can diminish its power to instill fear and erode confidence.
insights.quote: And as I get older, I find myself confronting failure on purpose as much as I canto try and become less fearful of it.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Ben Fogle - Overcoming My Lifelong Battle With Self-doubt

insights.text: resilience and hard work are not innate talents but can be learned and cultivated through exposure to strong leadership and positive examples.
insights.quote: Resilience and robustness and hard work can be taught and learned. I don't think it's something you're born with
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Gary Neville: From Football Legend To Building A Business Empire

insights.text: the best way to learn resilience is observing the actions of role models.
insights.quote: Learning through words those words that your dad would say to you about getting up and getting out every morning is a great way to learn but actions i think in hindsight seem to be the best way to to really learn those lessons vicariously from observing our parents and how they're behaving in their lives
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Gary Neville: From Football Legend To Building A Business Empire

insights.text: the intelligence cycle promotes continuous learning by transforming information into knowledge through experience.
insights.quote: What happens is you learn information. From that information, you develop knowledge, and then you test that knowledgethrough experience.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Manipulation Expert: CIA Spy Reveals You're Being Controlled! "You're Being Manipulated Without Realising It"! Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: agency is taking responsibility for how you interpret the world from now on, based on your current knowledge, not from past decisions made as a result of trauma.
insights.quote: Agency also means that if I have any kind of dysfunction or illness,it's not just that I put my fate in the hands of a physician or a healer, but I make the decisions.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Gabor Mate: The Childhood Lie That’s Ruining All Of Our Lives

insights.text: not everyone is suited for generating new ideas, and that's okay; the world needs people with different skill sets.
insights.quote: So there is going to be a cohort of people who just don't think of new ideas and they're probably really good at other things. And that's okay because the world needs all of them, right?
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 36 - How To Create Great Ideas: Marcia Kilgore

insights.text: analyzing past problems within the same emotions only reinforces imbalance in the brain.
insights.quote: When a person analyzes their problems within the emotions of the past, they make their brain worse. They actually drive it further out of balance. They're over arousing it.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Dr. Joe Dispenza: Secret To Living Without Fear & Anxiety Forever! Your Mind Can Heal Itself! Stress Is The Root Of 90% Of All Diseases!

insights.text: heart coherence, achieved through elevated emotions, signals the brain that trauma is over and resets the baseline.
insights.quote: When the heart gets coherent...it immediately informs the brain that the trauma is over. The heart tells the brain the past is over, the event is over, and it resets the baseline in the brain.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Dr. Joe Dispenza: Secret To Living Without Fear & Anxiety Forever! Your Mind Can Heal Itself! Stress Is The Root Of 90% Of All Diseases!

insights.text: the memory without the emotional charge is called wisdom.
insights.quote: The memory without the emotional charge is called wisdom.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Dr. Joe Dispenza: Secret To Living Without Fear & Anxiety Forever! Your Mind Can Heal Itself! Stress Is The Root Of 90% Of All Diseases!

insights.text: diversification is crucial for long-term financial success and mental health, acting as 'kevlar' against significant losses.
insights.quote: Diversify. It's such a bulletproof Kevlar for your mental health.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Investing Expert: We’re Raising The Most Unhappy Generation In History! The Unseen Link Between Marriage & Wealth! Hard Work Doesn't Build Wealth! - Scott Galloway

insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Physiotherapist: You Need To Know This About Creatine! Melt Belly Fat With One Change! The Hidden Cost Of Getting Shredded! - Jeff Cavaliere

insights.text: different types of exercise can target different brain functions; cardio improves mood and focus, while strength training enhances long-term memory.

insights.text: neuroplasticity allows the brain to adapt and change throughout life, meaning it is always possible to improve cognitive function through targeted interventions.
insights.quote: The brain is incredibly adaptable, and we have the power to change its structure and function through specific exercises and activities.
insights.episode_title: _summary

insights.text: small, consistent efforts, even just 10 minutes of exercise a day, can have a significant cumulative impact on brain health.
insights.quote: You don't need hours at the gym; even 10 minutes a day of movement can create noticeable positive changes.
insights.episode_title: _summary

insights.text: overthinking can be a significant barrier to confidence and success, particularly when internalizing external pressures to conform.
insights.quote: So I think actually a lot of that voice was internal. And actually I wish if I could go back in time, I wish I could kind of shake my shoulder, shake a young me on the shoulders and go, just don't overthink things, just chill out a little bit.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Ben Fogle - Overcoming My Lifelong Battle With Self-doubt

insights.text: awareness of the systemic forces that shape our beliefs and limit our potential is the first step to breaking free and living authentically.
insights.quote: The first thing I would say is awareness of the systemis quite a substantial stepbecause most of us are not aware of the system.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Manipulation Expert: CIA Spy Reveals You're Being Controlled! "You're Being Manipulated Without Realising It"! Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: breaking out of the 'shed' requires shattering the limiting beliefs imposed by external influences.
insights.quote: A lot of what I try to do with Everyday Spy is just shatter the glassbecause you don't need the glass to be between you and the real world.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Manipulation Expert: CIA Spy Reveals You're Being Controlled! "You're Being Manipulated Without Realising It"! Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: effective communication involves understanding both one's own perception and the other person's perspective to find common ground and build influence.
insights.quote: The only way that I canfind common ground with you is to stop thinking about what's happening around me from my perception and start thinking from your perspective.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Manipulation Expert: CIA Spy Reveals You're Being Controlled! "You're Being Manipulated Without Realising It"! Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: to gain perspective, one must quiet their sensory organs and actively listen to others, observing both their words and their delivery.
insights.quote: You get yourself into a place where your sensory organs can take a break...it gives you a higher level of awareness, a higher levelof observational skills.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Manipulation Expert: CIA Spy Reveals You're Being Controlled! "You're Being Manipulated Without Realising It"! Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: often those lessons you learn from psychedelics are more about showing you what you need, as opposed to what you might be thinking or wanting to experience.
insights.quote: There's a really deep insightful experience you get from psychedelics which you can often go in wanting a thing but it isn't what you need.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Overcoming Depression, Burnout, Anxiety and Insomnia with Dan Murray-Serter

insights.text: having someone who can confront power with truth is important because it prevents decisions from becoming based on inaccurate information or skewed perspectives.
insights.quote: Having people who can come into your office and go, John, that comment was just way out of line or really unhelpful...So people who can confront power with truth.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Building A Business Empire WITHOUT ANY Marketing: Five Guys CEO: John Eckbert

insights.text: authenticity thrives because social media promotes conformity and a single way of thinking.
insights.quote: Social media is still forcing us to conform. And that's againcreating a massive void. It's forcing us now to all be, speak, and even think the same at threatof being cancelled.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E45: I Have NEVER Shared This Before.

insights.text: understanding your 'why' is fundamental to sticking with a fitness journey.
insights.quote: Finding the why to get yourself on track with pursuing optimal health is everything.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Physiotherapist: You Need To Know This About Creatine! Melt Belly Fat With One Change! The Hidden Cost Of Getting Shredded! - Jeff Cavaliere

insights.text: neuroplasticity allows the brain to adapt and change throughout life, meaning it is always possible to improve cognitive function through targeted interventions.
insights.quote: The brain is incredibly adaptable, and we have the power to change its structure and function through specific exercises and activities.
insights.episode_title: _summary

insights.text: effective employee motivation involves paying fairly and well, then fostering autonomy, mastery, and purpose.
insights.quote: You got to pay people fairly and pay people well. And then you want to offer, exactly as you say, some autonomy, some control, some sovereignty over what you do, how you do it, when you do it, where you do it. Mastery, which is a chance to get better at something that matters, to make progress and meaningful work. And a sense of purpose.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Real Trick To Long Term Motivation: Daniel Pink

insights.text: purpose is twofold, encompassing both "capital p" (making a significant difference) and "small p" (making a contribution on a daily basis).
insights.quote: One kind of purpose is what I like to think of as capital P purpose, large P purpose... There's a second kind of purpose that I call small P purpose. And that's just making a contribution.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Real Trick To Long Term Motivation: Daniel Pink

insights.text: a fulfilling life consists of happiness, meaning, and psychological richness (variety).
insights.quote: The happiness is actually one of three factors that matter. So one is happiness. The second is meaning. A lot of things you do because it gives you meaning, and it doesn't necessarily give you happiness... And then there's a third factor that's also really interesting, which is called a psychological rich life, which is basically variety.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 151: Neuroscientist Reveals The 3 Things You Need To Do For A Fulfilling Life: Tali Sharot

insights.text: the key to equanimity is to purposefully rehearse success to combat anxiety.
insights.quote: I have to find a way to insert myself to break that rehearsal of failure and instead force myself to focus on rehearsing success, which it's almost silly, but it really does drop my anxiety to next to nothing.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 76 - The Number One Cause Of Anxiety Today: Tom Bilyeu

insights.text: the delayed stress reaction explains why people often feel worse *after* a stressful period ends, because psychological defenses come down and true emotions emerge as a deluge.
insights.quote: When that acute period of stress is over... then our psychological defenses come down and our true emotions can emerge. And it's often a deluge.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Mental Health Doctor: “Sitting Is Increasing Your Anxiety!”, Your Phone Is Destroying Your Brain, You May Have ‘Popcorn Brain’!

insights.text: the purpose of exercise is not just physical but has a mental health benefit.
insights.quote: People start exercising because those are all aspirational things.It's like they're out of reach.Most people who exercise, at least in my experience,who I have been able to convince to exercise,they exercise because of the mental health promise, not the physical health promise
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Mental Health Doctor: “Sitting Is Increasing Your Anxiety!”, Your Phone Is Destroying Your Brain, You May Have ‘Popcorn Brain’!

insights.text: true joy comes from random, meaningful moments and deep connection, not from external achievements or material possessions.
insights.quote: True joy doesn't come from the restaurant or from the holiday or from the going and doing the tv thing whatever it's actually really the joy comes from something that's actually very hard to predict it's like those really meaningful moments.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Mary Portas - How To Stop Living A Life That Isn't True To You

insights.text: kindness in business involves understanding a business's true purpose and creating an environment that values people and the planet, moving from a focus on 'me' to 'we'.
insights.quote: You create a different way of being a business that's not siloed,it's not individualistic. You move from me to we.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Mary Portas - How To Stop Living A Life That Isn't True To You

guests: Scott Galloway, Constantine Kisin, Daniel Priestley
Alex Hormozi, Cody Sanchez, Daniel Priestley

insights.text: creating 'emotional buttons' helps reconnect with positive emotions and manage anxiety by identifying triggers that lead to feelings of peace and happiness.
insights.quote: I need a place where I canget back to that. And I need that formula. So I call these emotional buttons.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Matthew Hussey: The Secret To Building A Perfect Relationship

insights.text: exercise is a potent tool for managing anxiety and improving resilience to stress.
insights.quote: Regular physical activity can build your brain's ability to handle stress, essentially making you more resilient.
insights.episode_title: _summary

insights.text: mindfulness practices, such as meditation, can complement exercise by further enhancing focus and reducing stress.
insights.quote: Combining physical exercise with mindfulness techniques amplifies the benefits for both cognitive function and emotional well-being.
insights.episode_title: _summary

insights.text: agency and control are crucial for well-being; restricted agency causes anxiety.
insights.quote: When we feel we have control, we have agency that enhances well-being. When we feel our agency has been restricted, that causes anxiety, right?
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 151: Neuroscientist Reveals The 3 Things You Need To Do For A Fulfilling Life: Tali Sharot

insights.text: high heart rate variability is correlated with better adaptation to environmental stress, and is modifiable to a degree.
insights.quote: Heart variability is kind of a measure of that ability to adapt to your environment in a functional way.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Fitness Scientist: "Even A Little Alcohol Is Hurting Your Health!", "Late Night Screen Time Linked To Cancer!", "Working Shifts Kills You 15 Years Early!" - Kristen Holmes

insights.text: diet plays a major role in anxiety reduction.
insights.quote: If you said you can make one change to somebody's life, what change would you make to lower their anxiety? 100% their diet.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 76 - The Number One Cause Of Anxiety Today: Tom Bilyeu

insights.text: meditation is crucial to interrupt rumination and anxiety spirals.
insights.quote: Meditation is the key for me to dealing with anxiety.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 76 - The Number One Cause Of Anxiety Today: Tom Bilyeu

insights.text: the key to equanimity is to purposefully rehearse success to combat anxiety.
insights.quote: I have to find a way to insert myself to break that rehearsal of failure and instead force myself to focus on rehearsing success, which it's almost silly, but it really does drop my anxiety to next to nothing.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 76 - The Number One Cause Of Anxiety Today: Tom Bilyeu

insights.text: social media significantly contributes to anxiety due to comparison and constant feedback.
insights.quote: I really do feel like if you removed social media, you'd remove a tremendous amount of anxiety.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 76 - The Number One Cause Of Anxiety Today: Tom Bilyeu

insights.text: courage involves doing things that induce anxiety, pushing beyond comfort zones and challenging pre-existing narratives.
insights.quote: Courage is doing the thing that you're afraid of. That is courage.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Manipulation Expert: CIA Spy Reveals You're Being Controlled! "You're Being Manipulated Without Realising It"! Andrew Bustamante

guests: Louise Perry, Erica Komisar, Deborah Frances-White

insights.text: economic security is paramount because a capitalist society is a generous place if you have money but a rapacious place if you don't.
insights.quote: America becomes more like itself every day. And that is, it's a generous, loving place if you have money. It's a rapacious, violent place if you don't.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Investing Expert: We’re Raising The Most Unhappy Generation In History! The Unseen Link Between Marriage & Wealth! Hard Work Doesn't Build Wealth! - Scott Galloway

insights.text: diversification is crucial for long-term financial success and mental health, acting as 'kevlar' against significant losses.
insights.quote: Diversify. It's such a bulletproof Kevlar for your mental health.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Investing Expert: We’re Raising The Most Unhappy Generation In History! The Unseen Link Between Marriage & Wealth! Hard Work Doesn't Build Wealth! - Scott Galloway

insights.text: wealthy individuals often utilize tax strategies that are not readily available or known to the average person.
insights.quote: The tax code has gone from 400 pages to 4,000, and that extra 3,600 pages are to turn rich people into super rich people.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Investing Expert: We’re Raising The Most Unhappy Generation In History! The Unseen Link Between Marriage & Wealth! Hard Work Doesn't Build Wealth! - Scott Galloway

insights.text: rather than trying to day trade, invest early and invest in the s&p 500
insights.quote: Low-cost index funds. It's a two-and-a-half-hour conversation. That concludes it. Put it in the S&P 500. Low-cost index funds.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Investing Expert: We’re Raising The Most Unhappy Generation In History! The Unseen Link Between Marriage & Wealth! Hard Work Doesn't Build Wealth! - Scott Galloway

insights.text: having a job can be a strategic advantage when starting a business, providing financial stability and reinvestment opportunities.
insights.quote: The utility of having a job whilst running the business is huge. One, because you can earn money to survive.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 44 - Ben Francis: Knowing This Will Accelerate Your Business

insights.text: small, consistent investments in health yield significant long-term benefits.
insights.quote: Even the smallest investments...will pay off, maybe not even right now, but as you started this with the idea of down the road, like you're realizing now at 32, it's going to matter at 52, 62, 72.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Physiotherapist: You Need To Know This About Creatine! Melt Belly Fat With One Change! The Hidden Cost Of Getting Shredded! - Jeff Cavaliere

insights.text: a fulfilling life consists of happiness, meaning, and psychological richness (variety).
insights.quote: The happiness is actually one of three factors that matter. So one is happiness. The second is meaning. A lot of things you do because it gives you meaning, and it doesn't necessarily give you happiness... And then there's a third factor that's also really interesting, which is called a psychological rich life, which is basically variety.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 151: Neuroscientist Reveals The 3 Things You Need To Do For A Fulfilling Life: Tali Sharot

insights.text: money is an illusion because it only exists when you spend it.
insights.quote: Most people don't understand that if I have a hundred pounds in the bank, I literally have nothing. I have nothing. The bank has my hundred pounds and the bank can decide whatever they want to do if they wanted to take it away from me.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Happiness Expert Returns: Retrain Your Brain For Maximum Happiness: Mo Gawdat

insights.text: it's important to find investors, advisors, or other founders who are willing to doubt your business in order to reveal potential issues and vulnerabilities.
insights.quote: A really smart thing I've heard, which I didn't do... is to sort of pick out the smartest investors you can, or smartest advisors or other founders, and ask them why you think your business will fail.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Monzo CEO On Death Threats, Depression & Digital Banking Wars - Tom Blomfield

insights.text: views not personally investigated and validated are likely illusions.
insights.quote: Everything that you haven't visited and investigated and arrived at a competent, confident conviction that this is your own view is probably an illusion, okay?
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 139: "Your Inner Voice Is Limiting You!" How To Control The Negative Inner Thoughts: Mo Gowdat

guests: Benjamin Weinthal, Annie Jacobsen, Andrew Bustamante
Scott Galloway, Constantine Kisin, Daniel Priestley
Roaul Pal, Humphrey Yang, Jaspreet Singh
Alex Hormozi, Cody Sanchez, Daniel Priestley
Stacey Sims, Natalie Crawford, Mary Claire Haver, Vonda Wright

insights.text: the most interesting person is the most interested person in the room; ask questions that make people think, not autopilot.
insights.quote: The most interesting person in a room tends to be the most interested person in the room...Challenge yourself to be the outlier. And think of a question you can ask someone...that throws them out of autopilot.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Oz Pearlman (Mentalist): This Small Mistake Makes People Dislike You! They Do This, They’re Lying!

insights.text: agency and control are crucial for well-being; restricted agency causes anxiety.
insights.quote: When we feel we have control, we have agency that enhances well-being. When we feel our agency has been restricted, that causes anxiety, right?
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 151: Neuroscientist Reveals The 3 Things You Need To Do For A Fulfilling Life: Tali Sharot

insights.text: prioritizing sleep and well-being enhances decision-making and overall performance.
insights.quote: I'm judged by the quality of my decisions, not the quantity of my decisions.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Arianna Huffington: I Was Lying In A Little Pool Of Blood! The Huffington Post, A $1 Billion 'Flop'!

insights.text: consistent sleep-wake timing is more crucial for longevity than sleep duration.
insights.quote: It is the degree to which you stabilize when you go to bed and when you wake up that predicts mortality.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Fitness Scientist: "Even A Little Alcohol Is Hurting Your Health!", "Late Night Screen Time Linked To Cancer!", "Working Shifts Kills You 15 Years Early!" - Kristen Holmes

insights.text: sleep debt in leaders negatively impacts the psychological safety of their team.
insights.quote: For every 45 minutes of sleep that the leader had, there was a significant decrease in psychological safety of the direct reports.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Fitness Scientist: "Even A Little Alcohol Is Hurting Your Health!", "Late Night Screen Time Linked To Cancer!", "Working Shifts Kills You 15 Years Early!" - Kristen Holmes

insights.text: sex with a partner, but not masturbation, before bed is linked to better sleep and recovery markers.
insights.quote: People who are reporting that they're having sex before bed...with a partner have better markers of sleep and recovery... We don't see the same effect when folks are having sex without a partner and reporting it.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Fitness Scientist: "Even A Little Alcohol Is Hurting Your Health!", "Late Night Screen Time Linked To Cancer!", "Working Shifts Kills You 15 Years Early!" - Kristen Holmes

insights.text: insufficient sleep can cause you to lose more muscle than fat when dieting.
insights.quote: If you're not getting sufficient sleep, then 60% of all of the weight that you lose will come from lean muscle mass and not fat.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Matthew Walker: The World’s No.1 Sleep Expert (The 6 Sleep Hacks You NEED!)

insights.text: caffeine masks sleepiness by blocking adenosine, but the sleepiness chemical continues to build up, leading to a 'caffeine crash'.
insights.quote: Caffeine races in it latches onto the adenosine receptors and blocks the signal of adenosine...when the caffeine finally gets metabolized...it's that plus all of the adenosine sleepiness that's been building up during that time in between.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Matthew Walker: The World’s No.1 Sleep Expert (The 6 Sleep Hacks You NEED!)

insights.text: sleep deprivation significantly increases cravings for unhealthy foods by impacting hormone levels, reward centers, and endocannabinoid production.
insights.quote: What they found is that when you are underslept,you eat more of everything, but you especially eat moreof these heavy hitting stodgy carbohydrates,bread, pasta, pizza.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Matthew Walker: The World’s No.1 Sleep Expert (The 6 Sleep Hacks You NEED!)

insights.text: dreaming during rem sleep is essential for emotional processing, allowing the brain to strip away the emotional intensity of difficult experiences.
insights.quote: Dream sleep does is then it takes that useful emotional memory and it will detox the emotion from the memory. It strips the bitter emotional rind from the informational orange.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Matthew Walker: The World’s No.1 Sleep Expert (The 6 Sleep Hacks You NEED!)

guests: Roaul Pal, Humphrey Yang, Jaspreet Singh
Alex Hormozi, Cody Sanchez, Daniel Priestley

insights.text: women often enjoy erotic and adventurous sex, contradicting the societal expectation of tame or romantic encounters.
insights.quote: So much research now shows that women like erotic wild sex.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 128: How To Fix Your Sexless Relationship: Tracey Cox

insights.text: when building a business, it is important to build in time for reflection and joy.
insights.quote: It isn't just about achieving. It's about joy while you're achieving.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Arianna Huffington: I Was Lying In A Little Pool Of Blood! The Huffington Post, A $1 Billion 'Flop'!

insights.text: a 48-hour fast can reboot the dopamine system, potentially reducing food cravings and increasing enjoyment of food with less consumption.
insights.quote: When people go without food for 48 hours, the whole dopamine system will be rebooted... new dopamine receptor sites appeared. So that when they brought food back in to the equation, they actually got more enjoyment out of food with less food.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Top 7 Belly Fat Burning Hacks For 2024 That Are PROVEN To Work!

insights.text: atypical burnout manifests as an *inability* to disconnect from work, even if one still enjoys it, and is often missed because it doesn't resemble classic burnout symptoms.
insights.quote: In one study, 60% of people with burnout had an inability to disconnect from work as their main feature of burnout... You could be that person who is engaged in work and can't shut off. And you're thinking to yourself, this can't be burnout. I'm totally, you know, engaged in work. In fact, I can't shut off my brain. You might be experiencing atypical burnout.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Mental Health Doctor: “Sitting Is Increasing Your Anxiety!”, Your Phone Is Destroying Your Brain, You May Have ‘Popcorn Brain’!

insights.text: people often get promoted into management positions where they oversee the work they once enjoyed doing, which can lead to dissatisfaction.
insights.quote: A lot of what happens in promotion, especially professionally, is you become a manager and you manage people who do the thing you love instead of doing the thing you love.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Psychology Master: The Colour That Makes You Attractive, How Your Name Determines Your Success & How To Become UNSTUCK In Your Marriage, Job, or Life!

insights.text: true joy is long-term and sustainable, originating from within, not external circumstances.
insights.quote: Whereas I've been happy at differentpoints in my life, but I hadn't ever experienced joy. And to me, the difference in happiness andbeing joyous, joyous is long-term and sustainable. And it doesn't come from anything external. It comes from here.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Lucy Hale Opens Up For The First Time About Eating Disorders, Relationships & Addiction

insights.text: true joy comes from random, meaningful moments and deep connection, not from external achievements or material possessions.
insights.quote: True joy doesn't come from the restaurant or from the holiday or from the going and doing the tv thing whatever it's actually really the joy comes from something that's actually very hard to predict it's like those really meaningful moments.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Mary Portas - How To Stop Living A Life That Isn't True To You

insights.text: authenticity and being true to oneself are essential for joy and fulfillment, even if it means going against societal expectations or norms.
insights.quote: The number one regret of the dying as she writes in her blog was quote not living a life true to myself
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Mary Portas - How To Stop Living A Life That Isn't True To You

insights.text: balance is key. don't be afraid to indulge in some things you enjoy once in a while.
insights.quote: Me taking care of myself involves me having a blowout once in a while on my friends and and suffering the hangover that comes with it to remind myself why I don't do it all the time
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Professor Green - How To Overcome Life’s Hardest Challenges & Find A Purpose

insights.text: the worst advice you can take is to believe that someone else can make you happy.
insights.quote: The worst advice I've ever received is that someone else can make you happy.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Mel Robbins: "Saying These 2 Words Will Fix Your Anxiety!" The New Trick For Stress, Anxiety & Breaking Every Bad Habit In 2024!

insights.text: purpose is twofold, encompassing both "capital p" (making a significant difference) and "small p" (making a contribution on a daily basis).
insights.quote: One kind of purpose is what I like to think of as capital P purpose, large P purpose... There's a second kind of purpose that I call small P purpose. And that's just making a contribution.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Real Trick To Long Term Motivation: Daniel Pink

insights.text: deliberately setting aside quiet time daily can help you connect with your potential.
insights.quote: To have that quiet period every day where you just sit not with not with ideas I'm going to think about this or I need to plan this or I need to work out what my passion is but just sit and just disconnect from that pull of the external eyes closed and just watch.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 70 - How Jonny Wilkinson Made It To The Top

insights.text: creating rigid routines or rituals can lead to *more* freedom, not less, by reducing the amount of mental energy and time spent dealing with the consequences of lacking structure.
insights.quote: This particular bit of rigidness has created more space not not less space for me.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Overcoming Depression, Burnout, Anxiety and Insomnia with Dan Murray-Serter

insights.text: the 'critical decade' for health is 35-45, during which establishing healthy habits and getting baseline labs are crucial for preventing future decline.
insights.quote: I think the critical decade for most of our health, Stephen,is no later than 35 to 45 for men and women.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Ageing Doctor: These Are They Early Signs Of Arthritis! If You Run & Don't Do This, Start Now! The Secret Cause of Alzheimer's!

insights.text: consistency, even in small actions, yields significant results over time, like compounding interest.
insights.quote: The small things repeated over time are much more powerful than the singular big decisions we make.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Everything 2020 Taught Me (part 2)

insights.text: routine, while providing structure, can stifle creativity and reduce life's overall excitement.
insights.quote: Surely the joy of life is exploration,being spontaneous, meeting new people,stumbling across new things that you didn't expect to stumble across,unplanned adventures,and being free enough to be continually inspiredby new things.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E38: A Simple Mindset Secret + Less Routine, More Life

insights.text: habits are deeply ingrained neurological pathways that are difficult to erase completely, but they can be replaced.
insights.quote: The original habit had never ever really been forgotten. It was always lurking somewhere there in their brain.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - 5 Scientific Rules for Making & Breaking Habits in 2023!

insights.text: stress significantly undermines willpower and the ability to delay gratification, making it harder to form new habits.
insights.quote: You're more likely to do the thing you don't want to do when you're stressed out.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - 5 Scientific Rules for Making & Breaking Habits in 2023!

insights.text: focusing on *stopping* a bad habit can backfire; instead, focus on *replacing* it with a new, healthier habit.
insights.quote: If you're a smoker and you tell yourself not to smoke your brain still hears smoke conversely if you tell yourself to chew gum every time you want a cigarette,your brain has a more positive action orientated goal to focus on.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - 5 Scientific Rules for Making & Breaking Habits in 2023!

insights.text: having a deeply personal and meaningful reason (intrinsic motivation) for changing a habit is essential for long-term success.
insights.quote: Change happens when the pain of staying the same becomes greater than the pain of making a change.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - 5 Scientific Rules for Making & Breaking Habits in 2023!

guests: Roaul Pal, Humphrey Yang, Jaspreet Singh
Professor Steve Peters and Nir Eyal

insights.text: exercise has immediate and long-term benefits for brain function, including mood, focus, and memory.
insights.quote: Even just one workout can immediately improve your mood and focus for the next two hours, and repeated exercise changes the brain's physiology for the better.
insights.episode_title: _summary

insights.text: exercise is a potent tool for managing anxiety and improving resilience to stress.
insights.quote: Regular physical activity can build your brain's ability to handle stress, essentially making you more resilient.
insights.episode_title: _summary

insights.text: different types of exercise can target different brain functions; cardio improves mood and focus, while strength training enhances long-term memory.
insights.quote: Cardio is fantastic for improving mood and your ability to focus, while incorporating some strength training can be particularly beneficial for long-term memory formation.
insights.episode_title: _summary

insights.text: small, consistent efforts, even just 10 minutes of exercise a day, can have a significant cumulative impact on brain health.
insights.quote: You don't need hours at the gym; even 10 minutes a day of movement can create noticeable positive changes.
insights.episode_title: _summary

insights.text: mindfulness practices, such as meditation, can complement exercise by further enhancing focus and reducing stress.
insights.quote: Combining physical exercise with mindfulness techniques amplifies the benefits for both cognitive function and emotional well-being.
insights.episode_title: _summary

insights.text: cold water immersion strips fat off your body faster than any type of exercise, due to the production of cold shock proteins.
insights.quote: If you want to strip fat off your body, get in cold water three to six minutes a day.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Top 7 Belly Fat Burning Hacks For 2024 That Are PROVEN To Work!

insights.text: the purpose of exercise is not just physical but has a mental health benefit.
insights.quote: People start exercising because those are all aspirational things.It's like they're out of reach.Most people who exercise, at least in my experience,who I have been able to convince to exercise,they exercise because of the mental health promise, not the physical health promise
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Mental Health Doctor: “Sitting Is Increasing Your Anxiety!”, Your Phone Is Destroying Your Brain, You May Have ‘Popcorn Brain’!

insights.text: even small increases in physical activity, especially for those starting from a sedentary lifestyle, yield significant cardiovascular and brain health benefits.
insights.quote: The biggest bang for your buck from a public health standpoint are people who are starting from almost zero and just starting to exercise a little bit... getting four or 5,000 steps per day is going to give you a big cardiovascular benefit and a big benefit for your brain.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Brain Professor: "Popular Treat Now Considered Deadlier Than Smoking!", "Alcohol Is Shrinking Your Brain" - David Raichlen

insights.text: combining physical activity with cognitive challenges, such as spatial navigation, can enhance the benefits of exercise on cognitive performance.
insights.quote: If you combine physical activity with cognitive challenges, you can boost the effects of physical activity on the brain, on especially cognitive performance.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Brain Professor: "Popular Treat Now Considered Deadlier Than Smoking!", "Alcohol Is Shrinking Your Brain" - David Raichlen

insights.text: low-intensity physical activity, even the kind that requires small shifts in your body is better than sitting still for long periods.
insights.quote: They're resting in postures that require a little muscle activity... it's possible that just that kind of low level of muscle activity is how our bodies are well adapted to rest.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Brain Professor: "Popular Treat Now Considered Deadlier Than Smoking!", "Alcohol Is Shrinking Your Brain" - David Raichlen
